title: Pure Curiosity: Zero Preference, Only Exploration

runtime.expected_outcome: With preference_strength=0.0 and flat C, the agent picks the action with the highest expected info gain. Trajectory visits the richest branch before any goal bias kicks in.

symbols.epistemic value: negative KL between posterior and prior predictive beliefs

audiences.kid: No snacks anywhere; the agent just wanders toward whichever path teaches it the most.
audiences.real: Setting preference_strength=0 makes the pragmatic term flat. All remaining signal comes from info-gain; the agent becomes pure curious.
audiences.equation: min G reduces to max expected info gain. The policy posterior concentrates on the most informative action sequence.
audiences.derivation: Under flat C, -ln p(o) is constant, so the pragmatic term is policy-invariant. Minimising G reduces to maximising expected KL (Schmidhuber 1991; Schwartenbeck et al. 2019).

orchestrate.objective: Demonstrate curiosity-only policy selection under flat preferences.
orchestrate.context: forked_paths with C-preference-override set to a zero vector (flat C).

credits: Parr, Pezzulo & Friston (2022), Active Inference, MIT Press (CC BY-NC-ND), Ch 4.
